step.title: Draft and Align
step.goal: Present the Structure Outline draft to the user for confirmation or adjustment. Loop on adjustments until the outline is locked. Firm checkpoint per spec §3.3.

substep.title: Present Outline
substep.ask:
Present the user with:
Structure outline proposal:
{{structure_outline_draft}}
Does this structure work for the ticket?
Options: - Confirm (Recommended if it looks right): lock the outline and proceed to ticket-plan - Add a section: I'll insert a section you propose - Remove a section: drop a recipe section we don't need - Reorder: change the section sequence - Add/remove Parts: toggle the Part-grouping decision - Reshape entirely: scrap the draft and propose a custom outline from scratch - Loop back to ticket-design: this exposes a design gap that needs revisiting

check.action: Apply the adjustment to the draft. Re-loop to substep 3a to re-present. Cap on iterations: 5; if the outline isn't locking after 5 rounds, surface this and ask the user whether to switch to fully-custom mode or loop back to ticket-design.
Discard {{structure_outline_draft}}. Re-enter step 2 with shape_mode = "fully-custom" forced. Draft a fresh outline. Then re-loop to substep 3a.

Append a note to the questions file: "Structure phase loop-back: design gap surfaced — {{specifics}}. User invoked ticket-design re-entry from ticket-structure step 3."
Stamp last_updated on the questions file. Exit this skill cleanly. User invokes ticket-design via menu (TD); that skill will see the new note and re-align.
NOTE: Looping back to ticket-design does NOT increment ticket-design's strike counter, because the loop-back is STRUCTURE-DRIVEN, not a foundational reversal. The strike counter only triggers on reversals of LOCKED foundational decisions, not on adjustments surfaced during structuring.

substep.title: Display Alignment Status

section.title: Iteration cap is a soft warning, not a hard limit
section.description: 5 iterations on the outline suggests the agent is missing a load-bearing concern (each adjustment is revealing a new gap). At 5 rounds, surface the pattern to the user and recommend either fully-custom mode or loop-back to ticket-design. Continuing past 5 without escalation produces a structure that keeps shifting; eventually the user will accept something that doesn't actually fit.

section.title: Loop-back vs strike counter
section.description: Looping back to ticket-design from this skill is NORMAL when a structure attempt reveals a design gap. It does NOT count as a foundational reversal because the user is not reversing a previously-locked decision — they are surfacing a need that wasn't visible in the Design Summary. ticket-design step 4 classifies as 'redirect_scope_ungrilled' if appropriate, which does not increment strikes.

section.title: Reshape is more expensive than adjust
section.description: Full reshape (scrap and restart) is costly — it discards work. Use only when the agent realizes the recipe was the wrong framework for this ticket. Most cases of "this doesn't feel right" are better served by add/remove/reorder iterations on the current draft.
